raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2930 requires developers and users of AI tools to mitigate and assess automated decision tools (ADTs) that make consequential decisions.",
    "AI decisions are determining whether people can buy a home or what medical care they receive.",
    "There is no check or accountability for the bias embedded in algorithms used for AI decisions.",
    "AB 2930 mandates developers and users of ADTs to conduct and record impact assessments including intended use, data makeup, and statistical analysis rigor.",
    "The data reported must include an analysis of potential adverse impacts based on race, color, ethnicity, sex, religion, age, national origin, or other protected classifications.",
    "Automated decision tools have traditionally been used for credit decisions but are now expanding to employment screening, insurance eligibility, and healthcare decisions.",
    "ADTs have been found to exhibit biases that lead to discriminatory impacts and harm marginalized communities.",
    "A study published in Science showed that a clinical algorithm for determining patient care was racially biased against Black patients.",
    "Without regulation, AI tools may reinforce inequality without accountability."
  ]
}
```
